name: Pre-commit and Pre-push Hook Setup with Husky
description: Automate code quality checks with Git hooks using husky and lint-staged to run linters, formatters, and tests before commits and pushes.
trendingReason: Shift-left testing practices are making pre-commit hooks standard in professional workflows to catch issues before they reach CI.
prerequisites: A Git repository with a package.json
ESLint, Prettier, or another linter configured

steps.title: Install husky and lint-staged
steps.description: Install husky for managing Git hooks and lint-staged for running linters on only staged files, keeping hooks fast.

steps.title: Initialize husky
steps.description: Sets up the .husky directory and adds a prepare script to package.json so hooks are installed automatically when anyone runs npm install.

steps.title: Configure lint-staged in package.json
steps.description: Define which commands to run on staged files by file extension. This runs ESLint and Prettier on JS/TS files, and Prettier alone on JSON/MD/CSS.
steps.tip: Lint-staged only processes files that are staged for commit, making hooks fast even in large repositories.

steps.title: Create the pre-commit hook
steps.description: Create a pre-commit hook that runs lint-staged. This will lint and format only the files being committed.

steps.title: Create a pre-push hook for tests
steps.description: Add a pre-push hook that runs the test suite before allowing a push. The --bail flag stops at the first failure for fast feedback.
steps.warning: Pre-push hooks that run a full test suite can be slow. Consider running only critical tests or tests related to changed files.

steps.title: Test the hooks with a commit
steps.command:
echo '// test' >> src/index.ts
git add src/index.ts
git commit -m "test: verify pre-commit hooks"
steps.description: Make a small change and commit to verify the pre-commit hook triggers lint-staged, which runs ESLint and Prettier on the staged file.
steps.tip: If a hook fails, the commit is aborted. Fix the issues reported by the linter and try committing again.

fullScript:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# git-hooks-setup.sh — Set up husky + lint-staged hooks
set -euo pipefail

echo "Installing husky and lint-staged..."
npm install --save-dev husky lint-staged

echo ""
echo "Initializing husky..."
npx husky init

echo ""
echo "Configuring lint-staged in package.json..."
node -e "
const pkg = require('./package.json');
pkg['lint-staged'] = {
  '*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}': ['eslint --fix', 'prettier --write'],
  '*.{json,md,css,yml}': ['prettier --write']
};
require('fs').writeFileSync('package.json', JSON.stringify(pkg, null, 2) + '\n');
"

echo ""
echo "Creating pre-commit hook (lint-staged)..."
echo 'npx lint-staged' > .husky/pre-commit

echo ""
echo "Creating pre-push hook (tests)..."
echo 'npm test -- --bail --watchAll=false' > .husky/pre-push

chmod +x .husky/pre-commit .husky/pre-push 2>/dev/null || true

echo ""
echo "Git hooks setup complete."
echo ""
echo "Hooks installed:"
echo "  pre-commit: lint-staged (ESLint + Prettier on staged files)"
echo "  pre-push:   npm test (full test suite)"
echo ""
echo "To skip hooks in an emergency: git commit --no-verify"

faq.question: How do I bypass hooks when I need to commit quickly?
faq.answer: Use 'git commit --no-verify' or 'git push --no-verify' to skip hooks. This should be used sparingly and only in genuine emergencies, as it defeats the purpose of having hooks.

faq.question: Will hooks slow down my commits?
faq.answer: Lint-staged only processes staged files, not the entire codebase, so pre-commit hooks are typically fast (1-5 seconds). Pre-push hooks running tests may be slower. Consider using --bail and running only affected tests.

faq.question: Do team members need to set up hooks manually?
faq.answer: No. When husky is installed with a 'prepare' script in package.json, hooks are automatically set up when anyone runs 'npm install'. The hooks themselves are committed to the repository in the .husky directory.
